terms.definition: The Hindi alphabet system. 'Varna' means letter and 'Mala' means garland or chain. So Varnamala is the complete chain of all Hindi letters, including both Swar and Vyanjan.
terms.exampleUsage: Hindi ki Varnamala mein Swar aur Vyanjan dono hote hain (The Hindi Varnamala has both vowels and consonants).

terms.definition: The vowels of Hindi. These are sounds that can be spoken freely on their own without the help of any consonant. There are 11 Swar in Hindi: अ, आ, इ, ई, उ, ऊ, ऋ, ए, ऐ, ओ, औ.
terms.exampleUsage: अ, आ, इ, ई, उ, ऊ yeh sab Swar hain (These are all vowels).

terms.definition: The consonants of Hindi. These are letters that need the support of a Swar to be spoken. There are 33 main Vyanjan in Hindi, from क to ह.
terms.exampleUsage: क, ख, ग, घ yeh sab Vyanjan hain (These are all consonants).

terms.definition: The symbol or sign of a Swar that is attached to a Vyanjan when they combine. Each Swar has its own Matra sign. For example, the Matra of आ is ा, of इ is ि, and of उ is ु.
terms.exampleUsage: का mein आ ki Matra lagi hai (In का, the Matra of आ is used).

terms.definition: A group of five consonants in Hindi that are produced from the same part of the mouth. There are five Varg: Ka-varg, Cha-varg, Ta-varg (retroflex), Ta-varg (dental), and Pa-varg.
terms.exampleUsage: Ka-varg mein क, ख, ग, घ, ङ aate hain (Ka-varg contains the letters Ka, Kha, Ga, Gha, Nga).

terms.definition: Short vowels in Hindi. These are Swar that take less time to say. The Hrasva Swar are: अ, इ, उ, ऋ. They are also called Chhote Swar.
terms.exampleUsage: अ ek Hrasva Swar hai kyunki ise bolne mein kam samay lagta hai (अ is a short vowel because it takes less time to say).

terms.definition: Long vowels in Hindi. These are Swar that take more time to say compared to Hrasva Swar. The Deergha Swar are: आ, ई, ऊ, ए, ऐ, ओ, औ. They are also called Bade Swar.
terms.exampleUsage: आ ek Deergha Swar hai kyunki ise bolne mein zyada samay lagta hai (आ is a long vowel because it takes more time to say).

terms.definition: A nasal sound mark in Hindi, shown by the Chandrabindu (ँ) symbol placed above a letter. It means the sound comes partly from the nose. For example, आँख (eye), चाँद (moon).
terms.exampleUsage: चाँद mein Anunasik ka chinha (ँ) laga hai (The word Chaand has the Anunasik mark).

terms.definition: A small slanting line (्) placed below a consonant to show that the consonant has no vowel sound with it. For example, in the word विद्या, the द has a Halant showing it joins directly with य without any vowel in between.
terms.exampleUsage: क् mein Halant laga hai, iska matlab क ka Swar hat gaya hai (क् has a Halant, meaning the vowel sound of क is removed).

terms.definition: The script (writing system) used to write Hindi. It is written from left to right and has a horizontal line (Shirorekha) on top of each letter. Hindi, Sanskrit, and Marathi all use the Devanagari script.
terms.exampleUsage: Hindi Devanagari lipi mein likhi jaati hai (Hindi is written in the Devanagari script).
